segments.text: The sayings of Agur son of Jakeh—an inspired utterance. This man's utterance to Ithiel:
I am weary, God, but I can prevail.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: Surely I am only a brute, not a man; I do not have human understanding.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: I have not learned wisdom, nor have I attained to the knowledge of the Holy One.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: Who has gone up to heaven and come down? Whose hands have gathered up the wind? Who has wrapped up the waters in a cloak? Who has established all the ends of the earth? What is his name, and what is the name of his son? Surely you know!
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: Every word of God is flawless; he is a shield to those who take refuge in him.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Do not add to his words, or he will rebuke you and prove you a liar.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Two things I ask of you, Lord; do not refuse me before I die:
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: Keep falsehood and lies far from me; give me neither poverty nor riches, but give me only my daily bread.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: Otherwise, I may have too much and disown you and say, 'Who is the Lord?' Or I may become poor and steal, and so dishonor the name of my God.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: Do not slander a servant to their master, or they will curse you, and you will pay for it.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: There are those who curse their fathers and do not bless their mothers;
those who are pure in their own eyes and yet are not cleansed of their filth;
those whose eyes are ever so haughty, whose glances are so disdainful;
those whose teeth are swords and whose jaws are set with knives to devour the poor from the earth and the needy from among mankind.
The leech has two daughters. 'Give! Give!' they cry.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: There are three things that are never satisfied, four that never say, 'Enough!':
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: the grave, the barren womb, land, which is never satisfied with water, and fire, which never says, 'Enough!'
The eye that mocks a father, that scorns an aged mother, will be pecked out by the ravens of the valley, will be eaten by the vultures.
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: There are three things that are too amazing for me, four that I do not understand:
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: the way of an eagle in the sky, the way of a snake on a rock, the way of a ship on the high seas, and the way of a man with a young woman.
This is the way of an adulterous woman: She eats and wipes her mouth and says, 'I've done nothing wrong.'
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: Under three things the earth trembles, under four it cannot bear up:
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: a servant who becomes king, a godless fool who gets plenty to eat,
a contemptible woman who gets married, and a servant who displaces her mistress.
Four things on earth are small, yet they are extremely wise:
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: Ants are creatures of little strength, yet they store up their food in the summer;
hyraxes are creatures of little power, yet they make their home in the crags;
locusts have no king, yet they advance together in ranks;
a lizard can be caught with the hand, yet it is found in kings' palaces.
There are three things that are stately in their stride, four that move with stately bearing:
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: a lion, mighty among beasts, who retreats before nothing;
a strutting rooster, a he-goat, and a king secure against revolt.
If you play the fool and exalt yourself, or if you plan evil, clap your hand over your mouth!
segments.speaker: Agur

segments.text: For as churning cream produces butter, and as twisting the nose produces blood, so stirring up anger produces strife.
segments.speaker: Agur
